name: owasp-top-10
description: OWASP Top 10 security rules for web applications. Covers injection, authentication failures, sensitive data exposure, and security misconfiguration.

security_checks.name: SQL injection
security_checks.description: Detects raw SQL queries with string concatenation or interpolation instead of parameterized queries.
security_checks.detect: String concatenation or interpolation in SQL query strings; raw query methods without parameter binding.

security_checks.name: Command injection
security_checks.description: Detects shell command execution with unsanitized user input.
security_checks.detect: exec(), system(), shell_exec(), subprocess with shell=True, or backtick operators using variables derived from user input.

security_checks.name: XSS via unescaped output
security_checks.description: Detects unescaped user input rendered in HTML templates.
security_checks.detect: Raw/unescaped output directives ({!! !!}, |safe, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, v-html) with user-controlled data.

security_checks.name: Authentication bypass
security_checks.description: Detects endpoints or controllers missing authentication middleware.
security_checks.detect: Route definitions or controller classes without auth middleware; public methods in authenticated controllers.

security_checks.name: Weak cryptography
security_checks.description: Detects use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithms.
security_checks.detect: MD5, SHA1 for password hashing; DES, RC4, or ECB mode encryption; hardcoded IVs or salts.

security_checks.name: Security misconfiguration
security_checks.description: Detects common security misconfigurations in application settings.
security_checks.detect: Debug mode enabled in production configs; permissive CORS (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *); missing security headers; default credentials.
